title: Punishments
content: The punishments to which offenders are liable under the provisions of this Code are:

title: Commutation of sentence of death
content: The Federal Government may, without the consent of the offender, commute the punishment of death to any other punishment provided by this Code.

title: Commutation of sentence of imprisonment for life
content: The Federal Government may, without the consent of the offender, commute the punishment of imprisonment for life to imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding twenty-five years.

title: Power of Federal Government to commute
content: Nothing in sections 54 or 55 shall derogate from the right of the President or the Provincial Government to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remissions of punishment.

title: Repealed
content: [Repealed by the Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 1921].

title: Fractions of terms of punishment
content: In calculating fractions of terms of punishment, imprisonment for life shall be reckoned as equivalent to twenty-five years.

title: Repealed
content: [Repealed].

title: Repealed
content: [Repealed].

title: Repealed
content: [Repealed].

title: Repealed
content: [Repealed].

title: Repealed
content: [Repealed].

title: Amount of fine
content: Where no sum is expressed to which a fine may extend, the amount of fine to which the offender is liable is unlimited, but shall not be excessive.

title: Sentence of imprisonment for non-payment of fine
content: In every case of an offence punishable with imprisonment as well as fine, the Court may direct that in default of payment of the fine, the offender shall suffer imprisonment.

title: Limit to imprisonment for non-payment of fine
content: The term for which the Court directs the offender to be imprisoned in default of payment of a fine shall not exceed one-fourth of the term of imprisonment which is the maximum fixed for the offence.
sub_clauses: The imprisonment may be in addition to any other imprisonment to which the offender may have been sentenced.

title: Description of imprisonment for non-payment of fine
content: The imprisonment which the Court imposes in default of payment of a fine may be of any description to which the offender might have been sentenced for the offence.

title: Imprisonment for non-payment of fine, fine only
content: If the offence is punishable with fine only, the imprisonment which the Court imposes in default of payment of the fine shall be simple.
sub_clauses: The term shall not exceed the following scale: Two months when the amount of the fine does not exceed fifty rupees; four months when the amount does not exceed one hundred rupees; six months in any other case.

title: Imprisonment to terminate on payment of fine
content: The imprisonment which is imposed in default of payment of a fine shall terminate whenever that fine is either paid or levied by process of law.

title: Termination on payment of proportional part
content: If a part of the fine is paid or levied, the imprisonment shall terminate proportionately.

title: Fine leviable within six years
content: The fine, or any part thereof which remains unpaid, may be levied at any time within six years after the passing of the sentence.
sub_clauses: Death of the offender does not discharge from the liability any property which would after his death be legally liable for his debts.

title: Limit of punishment of offence made up of several offences
content: Where anything which is an offence is made up of parts, any of which parts is itself an offence, the offender shall not be punished with the punishment of more than one of such his offences.
sub_clauses: Unless it be so expressly provided.
Where the offender is guilty of an offence at one time of which an offence at another time is a part, he shall not be liable to punishment for more than one of them.

title: Punishment of person guilty of one of several offences
content: In all cases in which judgment is given that a person is guilty of one of several offences specified in the judgment, but it is doubtful of which of these offences he is guilty, the offender shall be punished for the offence for which the lowest punishment is provided.

title: Solitary confinement
content: Whenever any person is convicted of an offence for which the Court has power to sentence him to rigorous imprisonment, the Court may order that the offender shall be kept in solitary confinement.
sub_clauses: For any portion or portions of the imprisonment to which he is sentenced, not exceeding three months in the whole.

title: Limit of solitary confinement
content: In executing a sentence of solitary confinement, such confinement shall in no case exceed fourteen days at a time.
sub_clauses: With an interval between the periods of solitary confinement of not less duration than such period.
When the imprisonment awarded shall exceed three months, the solitary confinement shall not exceed seven days in any one month of the whole imprisonment awarded.

title: Enhanced punishment for certain offences
content: Whoever, having been convicted of an offence punishable under Chapter XII or Chapter XVII of this Code with imprisonment of either description for a term of three years or upwards, shall be guilty of any offence punishable under either of those chapters with like imprisonment, shall be subject to enhanced punishment.
sub_clauses: Liability to imprisonment for life, or to imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding ten years.
